zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Jane Kirkpatrick於1720年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加宾夕法尼亚州蘭卡斯特蘭卡斯特時，她的父親Hugh Blain Kirkpatrick24歲，母親Margaret Gregg23歲。她於大約1749年與George Clingan在美國賓夕法尼亞州結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1823年9月23日在美國賓夕法尼亞州約克享年103歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Jane Kirkpatrick于1720年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加宾夕法尼亚州蘭卡斯特蘭卡斯特时，她的父亲Hugh Blain Kirkpatrick24岁，母亲Margaret Gregg23岁。她于大约1749年与George Clingan在美国宾夕法尼亚州结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1823年9月23日在美国宾夕法尼亚州約克享年103岁。

nameMeanings.name: Kirkpatrick
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from either of two places in Dumfriess called Kirkpatrick or any of various other places in Scotland, all so named from the dedication of their church to Saint Patrick. The Scottish word kirk sometimes replaced Gaelic cill ‘church’ in placenames; compare Kilpatrick .

variants.name: Kilpatrick
meanings: Irish (Ulster): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Giolla Phádraig ‘son of the servant of (Saint) Patrick’ (Irish Pádraig). See also Fitzpatrick .
Scottish: habitational name from any of various places called in Gaelic as cill Padraig ‘church of (Saint) Patrick’. See also Kirkpatrick .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.description: Historical Boundaries: 1728: Chester, Pennsylvania Colony, British Colonial America 1729: Lancaster, Pennsylvania Colony, British Colonial America 1776: Lancaster, Pennsylvania, United States
Historical Boundaries: 1749: York, Pennsylvania Colony, British Colonial America 1776: York, Pennsylvania, United States
Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.
The Declaration of Independence was signed in Philadelphia on July 4, 1776. The liberty bell was first rung here to Celebrate this important document.

historicEvents.title: Movement to Washington D.C.
historicEvents.description: While the growth of the new nation was exponential, the United States didn’t have permanent location to house the Government. The First capital was temporary in New York City but by the second term of George Washington the Capital moved to Philadelphia for the following 10 years. Ultimately during the Presidency of John Adams, the Capital found a permanent home in the District of Columbia.

historicEvents.title: Harrisburg Becomes the State Capital
historicEvents.description: Harrisburg had important parts with migration, the Civil War, and the Industrial Revolution.
historicEvents.urlTitle: United States History-History of Harrisburg Pennsylvania

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Jane Kirkpatrick was born in 1720, in Lancaster, Lancaster, Pennsylvania, British Colonial America, her father, Hugh Blain Kirkpatrick, was 24 and her mother, Margaret Gregg, was 23. She married George Clingan about 1749, in Pennsylvania, United States. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 5 daughters. She died on 23 September 1823, in York, Pennsylvania, United States, at the age of 103.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Jane Kirkpatrick est née en 1720, à Lancaster, Lancaster, Pennsylvanie, Amérique coloniale britannique, son père, Hugh Blain Kirkpatrick, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Margaret Gregg, avait 23 ans. Elle a épousé George Clingan environ 1749, à Pennsylvanie, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée le 23 septembre 1823, à York, Pennsylvanie, États-Unis, à l’âge de 103 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Jane Kirkpatrick(이)가 1720년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 펜실베이니아 주 랭커스터 랭커스터에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hugh Blain Kirkpatrick(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Gregg(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 대략 1749년 미국 펜실베이니아 주에서 George Clingan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1823년 9월 23일 미국 펜실베이니아 주 요르크에서 103세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Jane Kirkpatrick nasceu em 1720, em Lancaster, Lancaster, Pennsylvania, British Colonial America, seu pai, Hugh Blain Kirkpatrick, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Gregg, tinha 23 anos. Ela casou-se com George Clingan aproximadamente 1749, em Pensilvânia, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em 23 de setembro de 1823, em York, Pensilvânia, Estados Unidos da América, com 103 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Jane Kirkpatrick родилась в 1720 г. в местности ланкастир, ланкастир, Пенсильвания, бритиш колоняль амирика, её отцу по имени Hugh Blain Kirkpatrick было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret Gregg, 23. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени George Clingan около 1749 г. в местности Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 23 сентября 1823 г. в местности ерк, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 103 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Jane Kirkpatrick nació en 1720, en Lancaster, Lancaster, Pensilvania, British Colonial America, su papá, Hugh Blain Kirkpatrick, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Margaret Gregg, tenía 23 años. Se casó con George Clingan aproximadamente 1749, en Pensilvania, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 23 de septiembre de 1823, en York, Pensilvania, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 103 años.
